title: chamaemelum nobile f. nudum
intro: Chamaemelum nobile f. nudum, also known as 'naked chamomile', is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial with white, daisy-like flowers and no prominent white or yellow florets, making it distinct from the more commonly known Roman chamomile (Chamaemelum nobile). The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects, which can help to calm digestive issues, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation. Chamaemelum nobile f. nudum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote a good night's sleep and reduce stress.
